verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (Cysteine-Rich Secretory Protein 1) is a secreted protein expressed predominantly in the epididymis and found on the sperm surface, where it plays roles in sperm-egg binding and fertilization. It lacks a transmembrane domain and is secreted, but on mature spermatozoa it binds stably to the sperm plasma membrane outer leaflet (adsorbed onto the sperm head/tail surface during epididymal transit in a wash-resistant manner). Expression is restricted to the male reproductive tract / spermatozoa — a narrow germline/reproductive lineage. This qualifies as contextual/tissue_restricted_surface: the protein reaches the outer leaflet of the PM but only in this specialized reproductive cell type.
